title: Official Inflation Data Reveals 48.6% in October
detail: Iran's Central Bank belatedly releases October 2025 inflation data showing consumer prices rose 48.6% year-over-year, the highest reading since the 2018-19 sanctions crisis. Independent economists estimate the real figure is closer to 60%, as the official basket underweights food and energy. The delayed December reading is projected at 42.2%, a slight dip attributed to seasonal base effects rather than genuine improvement.

media.caption: Iran's Finance Minister publicly blames the June 2025 war for accelerating the rial's collapse

title: Emami Gold Coin Surges to 127 Million Tomans
detail: The Emami gold coin, a traditional inflation hedge for Iranian households, hits 127 million tomans, up 40% since June. Middle-class families are converting savings to gold and foreign currency as confidence in the rial evaporates. The Tehran Gold and Jewelry Union reports record daily trading volumes, with some exchanges running out of physical inventory.

media.caption: Iranians rush to convert savings into gold and foreign currency as the rial collapses
